Records.authors: Bourjaily, Jacob L., McLeod, Andrew J., Spradlin, Marcus, von Hippel, Matt, Wilhelm, Matthias
Records.description: Ten-particle two-loop planar double-box integral in massless $\varphi^4$ theory, finite in $d=4$, expressed as elliptic integral over weight-3 polylogarithms. Dual-conformally invariant. Depends on 7 cross-ratios. First example of elliptic integrals in massless scattering amplitudes.

Records.authors: Kristensson, Alexander, Wilhelm, Matthias, Zhang, Chi
Records.description: 10-point 2-loop massless planar elliptic double box in $d=4$ ($\mathcal{N}=4$ SYM). 7 massless propagators with dual-conformal numerator. Weight 4, expressed in elliptic MPLs. Full symbol computed with 36 rational + 24 algebraic + 20 elliptic letters.

2-loop traintrack: the elliptic double-box. 2-loop double-box with 7 massless propagators, 10 massless external legs grouped into 6 momenta. Finite and dual-conformally invariant in $d=4$. Involves weight-3 hyperlogarithms integrated over an elliptic curve. Feynman parametric representation given explicitly in Eqs. (5)-(9).
Records.location: Eq. (1), Sec. Introduction, Sec. Feynman Parameterization

Records.description: Elliptic ladder family, elliptic-ladder_L1x1_massless-internal-dualconformal. Family 1 of arXiv:2301.07965 (I_{L,R}): the (L+R)-cell ladder chain whose two distinguished massless legs p5, p10 attach to the SAME central rung (L cells left, R right); 4 off-shell corner legs; I_{L,R} = I_{R,L}; total loops L+R; lowest member I_{1,1} = the elliptic double box. Evaluated to elliptic multiple polylogarithms on ONE fixed elliptic curve at all loop orders. The staggered family I_{L,M,R} (legs M rungs apart) is known but not seeded.

Traintrack family, traintrack_L2_massless-internal-offshell-external. Planar chain of L boxes (cells), massless internal lines. External legs at every vertex: 4 off-shell corners (distinct masses, the collapsed pairs of the paper's 2L+6 massless particles) + 2L-2 massless middle legs; L=1 is the four-mass box. The L-loop traintrack is a weight-(L+1) hyperlogarithm over a Calabi-Yau (L-1)-fold: L=1 is a box, L=2 elliptic, L=3 a K3 surface, L=4 a Calabi-Yau threefold.
